primary_root_cause: DDL migration acquired ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on the orders table for ~39 seconds during live traffic. The single in-flight db.query span (SELECT on orders) blocked behind the lock and held a Node.js worker pool slot for the full 39s, causing cascading worker pool queue timeouts across all DB-bound endpoints.
contributing_root_causes: No lock_timeout set on migration session — migration silently blocks production queries for full duration instead of failing fast
No non-blocking DDL strategy (e.g., CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY) or maintenance window policy for table-locking DDL

detail.trigger_signal: DDL migration acquires ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on orders table; in-flight db.query span duration spikes from <10ms baseline to ~39,000ms at exact incident onset
detail.failure_mode: Blocked db.query holds sole Node.js worker pool slot for 39s; all subsequent /db/recent-orders requests hit 50ms queue timeout; /checkout requests with 30s timeout queue and time out; instantaneous onset and recovery consistent with lock acquisition/release

recommended_actions: Set lock_timeout on migration sessions (SET lock_timeout = '2s') to fail fast instead of blocking production traffic
Use non-blocking DDL strategies (CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY, nullable column additions without DEFAULT)
Run table-locking DDL only during scheduled low-traffic maintenance windows
Add application-layer statement_timeout on DB connections so lock-waiting queries fail before holding worker pool slots

validation_extensions.first_symptom_signal: First /db/recent-orders request hitting worker pool queue timeout (50ms) at exact moment db.query span duration spikes
validation_extensions.key_discriminator: Single db.query span on orders table shows ~39,000ms duration (4000x above <10ms baseline) at incident onset; all errors are 'worker pool queue timed out'; worker_pool_in_use spikes to 16 and queue_depth spikes mid-incident; instantaneous onset and recovery pattern (no gradual degradation) confirms lock acquisition/release rather than resource exhaustion
red_herrings: High checkout 504s look like payment issue but payment.charge succeeds with normal latency throughout — fault is entirely in the DB access path
payment_429_total metric shows high value but is a frozen cumulative counter from prior runs, not incrementing during this incident
